opening: SUNDAY OF THE HOLY FATHERS OF THE FIRST SIX ECUMENICAL COUNCILS

troparia.label: Troparion (6): Angelic powers were upon Your tomb and the guards became like dead men; Mary stood before Your tomb
troparia.text:
seeking Your most pure body. You captured Hades without being overcome by it. You met the Virgin and granted life. O
Lord risen from the dead, glory be to You!

troparia.label: Troparion (8): O Christ our God, You are most glorified, for You established our fathers as lights to all the earth. Through
troparia.text:
them You led us to the true faith. O most merciful Lord, glory be to You!
Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit.

kondakia.label: Kontakion (8): The preaching of the apostles and the decisions of the Fathers have established the true faith of the Church,
kondakia.text:
which she wears as the garment of truth, fashioned from the theology on high. She justly governs and glorifies the great
mystery of worship.
Now and for ever and ever: Amen.

kondakia.label: Kontakion (6): With His life-giving hand, Christ our God, the Giver of life, raised all the dead from the murky abyss and
kondakia.text: bestowed resurrection upon humanity. He is for all, the Savior, the Resurrection and the Life, and the God of all.

prokeimenon.text: Verse: Unto You I will cry, O Lord, my God, lest You turn from me in silence.

epistle.text:
Reading of the Epistle of St. Paul to the Romans
(Rom 15,1-7)
Brethren: We who are strong ought to put up with the failings of the weak and not to please ourselves; let each of us please
our neighbor for the good, for building up. For Christ did not please himself; but, as it is written, “The insults of those who
insult you fall upon me.” For whatever was written previously was written for our instruction, that by endurance and by the
encouragement of the scriptures we might have hope. May the God of endurance and encouragement grant you to think in
harmony with one another, in keeping with Christ Jesus, that with one accord you may with one voice glorify the God and
Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. Welcome one another, then, as Christ welcomed you, for the glory of God.

verses: Verse (6): He who lives in the aid of the Most High, shall dwell under the protection of the God of heaven.

gospel.text:
(Mt 9,27-35)
At that time, as Jesus was passing by, two blind men followed Jesus, crying out, “Son of David, have pity on us!” When he
entered the house, the blind men approached him and Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I can do this?” “Yes, Lord,”
they said to him. Then he touched their eyes and said, “Let it be done for you according to your faith.” And their eyes were
opened. Jesus warned them sternly, “See that no one knows about this.” But they went out and spread word of him through
all that land. As they were going out, a demoniac who could not speak was brought to him, and when the demon was driven
out the mute person spoke. The crowds were amazed and said, “Nothing like this has even been seen in Israel.” But the
Pharisees said, “He drives out demons by the prince of demons.” Jesus went around to all the towns and villages, teaching in
their synagogues, proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom, and curing every disease and illness.

communionHymn:
Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise Him in the highest.
Exult, you just, in the Lord; praise from the upright is fitting.
